column1: # For M3-chunk-1 these operate on a single row/column at the pointer,
# without the range prompt (that arrives with POINT in a later cycle).
# /WIR inserts a row above the pointer (row 1 → row 2).
# After: row 1 is empty; prior row 1 values sit in row 2; row 2 values in row 3.
# /WDR deletes the current row (row 1, empty) — shifts everything back up.
# /WIC inserts a column at pointer (col B). B-column values shift to C.
# /WDC deletes the column at pointer (col B, empty). C shifts back to B.
